basics.name: Arshia Dutta
basics.label: PhD Researcher
basics.summary: PhD Researcher at Royal Holloway, University of London examining AI-driven technologies and their adverse impact on marginalised populations, with a focus on border control technologies and intimate partner violence.

highlights: Advisors: Prof. Rikke Jensen and Dr. Maryam Mehrnezhad
EPSRC Centre for Doctoral Training in Cybersecurity for the Everyday

work.name: Max Planck Institute for Security and Privacy
work.summary: Investigated how AI developers and practitioners engage with ethical frameworks in real-world settings.
highlights: Conducted semi-structured interviews with 20+ AI developers and practitioners across UK, Europe, US, and Canada
Performed qualitative analysis including transcript annotation and inductive coding
Investigated communication and interoperability between AI ethical frameworks

work.name: King's College London
work.summary: Co-led patent analysis and participatory governance for identifying risky AI patents.
highlights: Developed search strings and annotation protocols for security-related AI patents under EU AI Act taxonomies
Annotated 250+ patents for automated risk assessment training datasets
Conducted interviews with researchers in technology-centric civil society organisations

awards.title: EPSRC CDT Studentship in Cybersecurity for the Everyday
